## 1. Role Justification

The Senior Gameplay Engineer #2 focuses on UI systems architecture, input handling, animation integration, and networking for player-facing features. This is a P0 hire required for Stage 5 (Full Production). The role sits alongside Senior Gameplay Engineer #1 under Dmitri Volkov's direction, with complementary focus areas: while SG#1 owns core gameplay loops and meta systems, SG#2 owns everything the player directly interacts with on screen — UI frameworks, input pipelines, animation state machines, and real-time UI synchronization for multiplayer features.

This hire is critical for achieving the project's design fidelity targets. The UI framework must serve the target audience on mid-tier mobile devices with 60fps performance, requiring deep optimization expertise that only a senior specialist can provide.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 2 (UI Systems Design), 3 (Input/Animation Architecture), 5 (Player-Facing Feature Implementation)

---

# Position Specification Document

**Role:** Senior Gameplay Engineer #2 (G12)
**Role Family:** Engineering — Gameplay
**Seniority:** Senior (L2)
**Team:** Casual Games Studio — Engineering Division
**Reports To:** Dmitri Volkov (Senior Game Engineer)
**Priority:** P0
**Compensation Band:** $130K–$170K base
**Document ID:** PSD-2026-G12-001
**Generated:** 2026-04-10T08:00:00Z
**Generated By:** CHRO Office (Automated Pipeline)
**Recruitment Phase:** Phase 3A — Engineering Ramp

---

## 1. Role Justification

The Senior Gameplay Engineer #2 focuses on UI systems architecture, input handling, animation integration, and networking for player-facing features. This is a P0 hire required for Stage 5 (Full Production). The role sits alongside Senior Gameplay Engineer #1 under Dmitri Volkov's direction, with complementary focus areas: while SG#1 owns core gameplay loops and meta systems, SG#2 owns everything the player directly interacts with on screen — UI frameworks, input pipelines, animation state machines, and real-time UI synchronization for multiplayer features.

This hire is critical for achieving the project's design fidelity targets. The UI framework must serve the target audience on mid-tier mobile devices with 60fps performance, requiring deep optimization expertise that only a senior specialist can provide.

**Pipeline Stages Owned:** 2 (UI Systems Design), 3 (Input/Animation Architecture), 5 (Player-Facing Feature Implementation)

---

## 2. Required Competencies (weighted)

| Competency                        | Weight | Minimum Threshold | Assessment Method                          |
| --------------------------------- | ------ | ----------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| UI Systems Architecture           | 25%    | ≥ 4/5             | Coding challenge + architecture case study |
| Input Handling & Animation        | 20%    | ≥ 4/5             | Technical deep-dive interview              |
| Unity/Unreal Engine (Mobile Path) | 15%    | ≥ 4/5             | Technical interview + profiling exercise   |
| Networking / Multiplayer UI       | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | System design exercise                     |
| Mobile UI Optimization            | 15%    | ≥ 3/5             | Performance profiling exercise             |
| Team Collaboration & Mentoring    | 10%    | ≥ 3/5             | Behavioral interview                       |

---

## 3. Must-Have Qualifications

- 5+ years gameplay programming experience with focus on UI/animation systems
- UI systems architecture and implementation for mobile games (shipped titles required)
- Input handling across multiple platforms (touch, gamepad, keyboard)
- Animation system integration (state machines, blend trees, Spine 2D or equivalent)
- Networking for multiplayer UI features (client-side prediction, server reconciliation)
- Demonstrated experience with UI performance optimization on mobile hardware
- Experience mentoring mid-level engineers on UI best practices

---

## 4. Nice-to-Have Qualifications

- Experience with games serving 100M+ MAU
- Published technical writing or conference talks on UI optimization
- Experience with cross-platform UI frameworks
- Familiarity with accessibility in game UI (colorblind modes, text scaling)

---

## 5. Compensation Structure

| Component         | Range / Value                                |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| Base Salary       | $130K–$170K (target: $150K, 75th percentile) |
| Performance Bonus | Up to 12% of base (target: 10%)              |
| Equity            | 0.10%–0.18% (Senior tier)                    |
| Signing Bonus     | Up to $20K                                   |
| Relocation        | Up to $10K                                   |
| Conference Budget | $5K/year                                     |

---

## 6. Sourcing Channels (prioritized)

| Channel                               | Priority | Quality Weight | Target Candidates        |
| ------------------------------------- | -------- | -------------- | ------------------------ |
| King, Playdemic, Supercell alumni     | 1        | 1.5×           | UI/Animation specialists |
| GDC UI/UX Track speakers              | 2        | 1.3×           | Published practitioners  |
| Unity/Unreal open-source contributors | 3        | 1.2×           | Framework authors        |
| Referrals from Dmitri Volkov          | 4        | 1.5×           | Known quantity           |
| LinkedIn passive sourcing             | 5        | 1.0×           | Broad net                |

---

## 7. Assessment Battery

| Assessment                 | Format                      | Duration | Weight | Pass Threshold |
| -------------------------- | --------------------------- | -------- | ------ | -------------- |
| Coding Challenge           | 2 problems (UI/input focus) | 90 min   | 25%    | ≥ 4/5          |
| System Design              | UI framework architecture   | 45 min   | 20%    | ≥ 4/5          |
| Simulated Panel Interview  | AI panel, signal questions  | 45 min   | 25%    | ≥ 3.5/5        |
| Behavioral / Culture Add   | STAR method                 | 30 min   | 15%    | ≥ 3/5          |
| Portfolio / Public Signals | Shipped titles, talks       | —        | 15%    | ≥ 3.5/5        |

---

## 8. Success Criteria (30/60/90 Day Plan)

| Milestone   | Criteria                                                                                               |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Day 1**   | Engine access granted, dev environment running, repo access configured                                 |
| **Week 1**  | First PR merged (UI system architecture draft); introduced to Dmitri Volkov and team                   |
| **Month 1** | Core UI framework implemented with input handling pipeline; initial design review passed               |
| **Month 2** | Animation integration complete for first feature set; UI performance baseline established              |
| **Month 3** | Full UI framework operational; networking for co-op features functional; mentoring mid-level engineers |
